children.label: A
children.text: on , was a contract school under title XI of the Education Amendments of 1978 [ et seq.] and the tribe or tribal organization operating the school submits to the Secretary a written notice of election to receive a grant under this chapter;April 28, 198825 U.S.C. 2000

children.label: B
children.text: was a Bureau-operated school under title XI of the Education Amendments of 1978 [ et seq.] and has met the requirements of subsection (b);25 U.S.C. 2000

children.label: C
children.text: is a school for which the Bureau has not provided funds, but which has met the requirements of subsection (c); or

children.label: D
children.text: is a school with respect to which an election has been made under paragraph (2) and which has met the requirements of subsection (b).

children.heading: New schools
children.label: 2 New schools
children.text: Any application which has been submitted under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act [ et seq.] by an Indian tribe for a school which is not in operation on , shall be reviewed under the guidelines and regulations for applications submitted under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act that were in effect at the time the application was submitted, unless the Indian tribe or tribal organization elects to have the application reviewed under the provisions of subsection (b).25 U.S.C. 5301January 8, 2002

children.heading: Additional requirements for Bureau-funded schools and certain electing schools
children.label: b Additional requirements for Bureau-funded schools and certain electing schools
i
children.text: transfer operation of the school to the Indian tribe or tribal organization, if the Indian tribe or tribal organization is not already operating the school; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make a determination as to whether the school is eligible for assistance under this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary makes a determination that the school is eligible for assistance under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a school which is not being operated by the Indian tribe or tribal organization, whether to transfer operation of the school to the Indian tribe or tribal organization; and

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the school is eligible for assistance under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: shall transfer operation of the school to the Indian tribe or tribal organization, if the tribe or tribal organization is not already operating the school; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall determine that the school is eligible for assistance under this chapter, unless the Secretary finds by clear and convincing evidence that the services to be provided by the Indian tribe or tribal organization will be deleterious to the welfare of the Indians served by the school.

children.label: i
children.text: equipment;

children.label: ii
children.text: bookkeeping and accounting procedures;

children.label: iii
children.text: ability to adequately manage a school; or

children.label: iv
children.text: adequately trained personnel.

children.heading: Additional requirements for a school which is not a Bureau-funded school
children.label: c Additional requirements for a school which is not a Bureau-funded school
A
children.text: the Indian tribe or tribal organization that operates, or desires to operate, the school submits to the Secretary an application requesting a determination by the Secretary as to whether the school is eligible for assistance under this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary makes a determination that a school is eligible for assistance under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: By not later than the date that is 180 days after the date on which an application is submitted to the Secretary under paragraph (1)(A), the Secretary shall determine whether the school is eligible for assistance under this chapter.

children.label: I
children.text: the adequacy of facilities or the potential to obtain or provide adequate facilities;

children.label: II
children.text: geographic and demographic factors in the affected areas;

children.label: III
children.text: adequacy of the applicant’s program plans;

children.label: IV
children.text: geographic proximity of comparable public education; and

children.label: V
children.text: the needs as expressed by all affected parties, including but not limited to students, families, tribal governments at both the central and local levels, and school organizations.

children.label: I
children.text: geographic and demographic factors in the affected areas;

children.label: II
children.text: adequacy and comparability of programs already available;

children.label: III
children.text: consistency of available programs with tribal education codes or tribal legislation on education; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the history and success of these services for the proposed population to be served, as determined from all factors including, if relevant, standardized examination performance.

children.heading: Geographic proximity
children.label: C Geographic proximity
children.text: The Secretary may not make a determination under this paragraph that is primarily based upon the geographic proximity of comparable public education.

children.heading: Other information
children.label: D Other information
children.text: Applications submitted under paragraph (1)(A) shall include information on the factors described in subparagraph (B)(i), but the applicant may also provide the Secretary such information relative to the factors described in subparagraph (B)(ii) as the applicant considers appropriate.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: E Deadline
children.text: If the Secretary fails to make a determination under subparagraph (A) with respect to an application within 180 days after the date on which the Secretary received the application, the Secretary shall be treated as having made a determination that the tribally controlled school is eligible for assistance under the title and the grant shall become effective 18 months after the date on which the Secretary received the application, or on an earlier date, at the Secretary’s discretion.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Filing of applications and reports
children.label: d Filing of applications and reports

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: All applications and reports submitted to the Secretary under this chapter, and any amendments to such applications or reports, shall be filed with the education line officer designated by the Director of the Office of Indian Education Programs of the Bureau of Indian Affairs. The date on which such filing occurs shall, for purposes of this chapter, be treated as the date on which the application or amendment was submitted to the Secretary.

children.heading: Supporting documentation
children.label: 2 Supporting documentation
children.text: Any application that is submitted under this chapter shall be accompanied by a document indicating the action taken by the tribal governing body in authorizing such application.

children.heading: Effective date for approved applications
children.label: e Effective date for approved applications
children.text: Except as provided by subsection (c)(2)(E), a grant provided under this chapter, and any transfer of the operation of a Bureau school made under subsection (b), shall become effective beginning the academic year succeeding the fiscal year in which the application for the grant or transfer is made, or at an earlier date determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: state the objections in writing to the tribe or tribal organization within the allotted time;

children.label: B
children.text: provide assistance to the tribe or tribal organization to overcome all stated objections;

children.label: C
children.text: at the request of the tribe or tribal organization, provide the tribe or tribal organization a hearing on the record under the same rules and regulations that apply under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act [ et seq.]; and25 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: D
children.text: provide an opportunity to appeal the objection raised.

children.heading: Timeline for reconsideration of amended applications
children.label: 2 Timeline for reconsideration of amended applications
children.text: The Secretary shall reconsider any amended application submitted under this chapter within 60 days after the amended application is submitted to the Secretary.

children.heading: Report
children.label: g Report
children.text: The Bureau shall submit an annual report to the Congress on all applications received, and actions taken (including the costs associated with such actions), under this section at the same time that the President is required to submit to Congress the budget under .section 1105 of title 31
